# VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0003 - QUIC RFC 9001 1-RTT Key Update Lifecycle Verification

## Scope

Verify the future repeated 1-RTT key-update lifecycle for RFC 9001 Section 6 and Sections 6.1 through 6.6, including local and peer initiated updates, repeated Key Phase alternation, successor packet opening, duplicate and tampered transition rejection, current-phase acknowledgment gating, AEAD usage-limit update requests, old-key retention, and old-key discard synchronized with sender/recovery state. The current artifact does not claim that broader lifecycle is implemented.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0002
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0004
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0006
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0007
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0008

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0004
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005

## Verification Method

Planned requirement-home execution, negative transition tests, fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase and epoch transitions, BenchmarkDotNet coverage, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.

## Preconditions

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0008 remain the baseline RFC 9001 Section 6 requirements for QUIC Key Phase updates.
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005 define the promoted lifecycle, retention, ordering, and AEAD-limit policy boundary for implementation.
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0145 remains the implemented first-successor install prerequisite.
- The 9001-06-1rtt-key-update-lifecycle gap remains open until the repeated lifecycle and old-key discard requirements are implemented and proven.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the existing first-successor RFC 9001 Section 6 requirement-home tests as a regression baseline.
- Run the direct RFC 9001 Section 6.1 first-successor requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010.
- Run the direct first RFC 9001 Section 6.2 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004.
- Run the direct first next-receive-key RFC 9001 Section 6.3 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002.
- Run the direct first send-key ordering RFC 9001 Section 6.4 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003.
- Run the direct first old/current next receive-selection RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002.
- Add and run positive tests for repeated local-initiated and peer-initiated key updates that alternate the Key Phase bit across more than one epoch.
- Add and run negative tests for duplicate same-phase packets, stale older-epoch packets, tampered successor packets, packet-number-incoherent old-key packets, unacknowledged repeated local updates, premature old-key discard, and authentication failure rollback.
- Add and run tests that prove AEAD usage-limit counters request a local update through the same acknowledgment-gated lifecycle and enter the terminal stateless-reset-only boundary when key update is impossible or integrity limits are reached.
- Add fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase observation and packet-protection epoch transition boundaries.
- Run a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for successor derivation, epoch install, protected packet formatting, and opening in the repeated lifecycle path.
- Render SpecTrace Markdown from JSON and run the repo-local trace validation checks available for the touched artifacts.

## Expected Result

The eventual lifecycle proof shows that endpoints can repeatedly update 1-RTT packet-protection keys after handshake confirmation, use the Key Phase bit to identify and detect the active epoch, decrypt packets that carry a changed Key Phase, keep both endpoints synchronized, gate local repeated updates on current-phase acknowledgment, handle AEAD usage limits without bypassing that gate, retain old keys long enough for safe reordering, reject packet-number-incoherent old-key packets where required, and discard old 1-RTT material only with sender/recovery cleanup. Until then, this verification remains planned and the repository only claims the bounded first-successor floor.

## Status

Planned for the broader lifecycle. On 2026-04-23, the existing first-successor Section 6 regression guard passed 19 focused tests with the serialized `-m:1` run preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-key-update-boundary/. The direct Section 6.1 first-successor proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 with 9 focused tests and a 28-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 guard preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-first-successor-proof/. The direct Section 6.1 KDF proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0009 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 with 6 focused tests, a 15-test all-S6P1 guard, and a 34-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 guard preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-kdf-proof/. The direct Section 6.1 old-key retention proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0007 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0008 with 6 focused tests, a 21-test all-S6P1 guard, a 40-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p1-old-key-retention/. The direct Section 6.2 first peer-update proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002 with 7 focused tests, a 28-test Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 guard, a 47-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-peer-update-ack/. The direct Section 6.2 old-key ACK proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004 with 4 focused tests, a 55-test first-successor guard spanning Sections 6.1 through 6.5, a 74-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p2-old-key-ack/. The direct Section 6.3 next receive-key proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002 with 5 focused tests, a 33-test Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 guard, a 52-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p3-next-receive-keys/. The direct Section 6.4 first send-key ordering proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0002 with 7 focused tests, a 40-test Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 plus first Section 6.4 guard, and a 59-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 plus first Section 6.2 plus S6P3-0002 plus first Section 6.4 guard preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p4-send-key-ordering/. The direct Sections 6.4 through 6.5 first old-key receive-selection proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 with 7 focused tests, a 47-test first-successor guard spanning Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 slice, a 66-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-old-key-selection/. The direct Section 6.5 first higher-recovered next-key receive-selection proof covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 with 4 focused tests, a 51-test first-successor guard spanning Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 receive-selection slice, a 70-test combined Section 6 plus Section 6.1 through the first Section 6.5 guard, and a QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks Dry validation preserved under artifacts/verification/20260423-rfc9001-s6p5-next-key-selection/. The touched Markdown views render cleanly; repo-wide SpecTrace core validation still fails at the known 6125-error residual baseline. Repeated 1-RTT key-update cycles, unconfirmed consecutive peer-update error policy, receive-key selection beyond the first successor, AEAD-limit-triggered updates, and old 1-RTT key discard remain open under the gap ledger.

## Related Artifacts

- SPEC-QUIC-RFC9001
- ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0003
- WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0003
- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0040
- WI-QUIC-CRT-0042
- VER-QUIC-CRT-0042

## Current Evidence Boundary

- Current tests prove first-successor install, changed-Key Phase observation, tampered-packet rejection, duplicate same-phase rejection, successor write-key use, stable header-protection keys, updated-key protection for subsequent packets, pre-confirmation local-update rejection, bounded repeat-update rejection before any current-phase acknowledgment support exists, successor receive-key installation, retained next first-update receive material, retained old first-update 1-RTT material before and after new-key packet authentication, explicit retained cleanup, first peer-initiated next-key packet opening, next-Key-Phase guard for successor install, updated send-key ACK protection for peer-initiated updates, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for retained old-key ACK packets that acknowledge first-successor phase-1 sends, first-update send-key ordering, the first old/current next receive-selection floors for lower and higher recovered packet numbers, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for old-key packet-number ordering violations, QUIC `quic ku` successor traffic-secret derivation, and packet key/IV derivation from updated secrets.
- Current benchmarks cover the first-successor install and protected packet formatting/opening path.
- No evidence in this artifact claims receive-key selection across old/current/next epochs beyond the first successor, repeated key-update cycles after acknowledgment, unconfirmed consecutive peer-update error policy, AEAD-limit-triggered local updates, or old 1-RTT key discard with sender/recovery cleanup.
